title: Homers Odyssey

jokes.text: Mrs. Krabappel, I didn't unlock that door.
jokes.setup: Response to prison field trip mention
jokes.punchline: Bart's specific denial reveals exactly what went wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that reveals the disaster through Bart's defensive specificity - great character writing

jokes.text: Uh, sorry, little dudes. Party hardy was tardy.
jokes.setup: Bus driver arriving late
jokes.punchline: Otto's stoner slang explanation in rhyme
jokes.explanation: Solid character-based humor that establishes Otto's personality

jokes.text: Hey, Otto. Hey, Ottoman.
jokes.setup: Greeting the bus driver
jokes.punchline: Bart plays on Otto's name being short for Ottoman
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that fits Bart's mischievous character

jokes.text: This morning I woke up with this one. / Not till you're 14, my little friend.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about tattoos, Otto showing new one
jokes.punchline: Implication Otto got tattooed while blackout drunk, then age restriction advice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Otto's irresponsible lifestyle while being oddly responsible about age limits

jokes.text: Oh, please, Mrs. Krabappel, not next to Wendell. He pukes on every bus ride. No offense, Wendell.
jokes.setup: Bart pleading not to sit next to someone
jokes.punchline: Casually brutal honesty followed by polite afterthought
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart - brutally honest but with a veneer of politeness that makes it funnier

jokes.text: Oh.
jokes.setup: Wendell's reaction to being called out for puking
jokes.punchline: Simple dejected response
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction that adds to the cringe of Bart's comment

jokes.text: We all know the tragic story of the young man who stuck his arm out the window and had it ripped off by a big truck coming in the other direction. And I was that boy.
jokes.setup: Otto telling cautionary tale about bus safety
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was the victim of the accident he's warning about
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection joke with dark comedy and character integration. Otto's casual delivery of his own trauma is peak Simpsons

jokes.text: Look, there's our school again. / It's a shortcut, Mrs. K. Trust me.
jokes.setup: Students noticing they're passing the school again
jokes.punchline: Otto insisting his obviously wrong route is a shortcut
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Otto's incompetence and stubbornness

jokes.text: The song will be 'John Henry Was a Steel Drivin' Man.' / Can I pick the song? / No.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Krabappel threatening Bart with having to sing
jokes.punchline: Bart's hopeful question immediately shut down, plus oddly specific song choice
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction with Bart's optimism immediately crushed

jokes.text: They took Bart Simpson to the graveyard / And they buried him in the sand...
jokes.setup: Bart forced to sing John Henry song
jokes.punchline: He changes lyrics to be about himself in morbid way
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart turning punishment into performance, subverting the folk song with his own morbid version

jokes.text: Hey, Wendell, you made it, buddy.
jokes.setup: Bart congratulating Wendell after bus ride
jokes.punchline: Reference to earlier joke about Wendell always puking on bus rides
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Bart can be surprisingly supportive while referencing earlier setup

jokes.text: Smilin' Joe Fission. Hi, there, energy eaters.
jokes.setup: Introduction of cartoon mascot character
jokes.punchline: Name pun on nuclear fission plus overly cheerful greeting
jokes.explanation: Good parody of educational cartoon characters with atomic pun name

jokes.text: And these are rods of uranium 235. Hi, Rod. / Hi. Hey. Good to see ya. / Hey, you guys look hot. / Of course we're hot. We're radioactive.
jokes.setup: Cartoon character introducing uranium rods as characters
jokes.punchline: Play on 'hot' meaning both attractive and literally hot from radiation
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning pun that works well in the cartoon context

jokes.text: I'll just put it where nobody'll find it for a million years.
jokes.setup: Dealing with nuclear waste in cartoon
jokes.punchline: Casually dismissing serious environmental problem with cheerful tone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that exposes the absurdity of nuclear industry PR through cheerful dismissal of waste disposal

jokes.text: Hey, Bart, our dad says your dad is incompetent. / What does incompetent mean?
jokes.setup: Kid insulting Homer to Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart doesn't understand the insult
jokes.explanation: Good setup for next joke, showing Bart's innocence protecting him from insult

jokes.text: It means he spends more time yakking and scarfing down doughnuts than doing his job. / Oh, okay. I thought you were putting him down.
jokes.setup: Kid explaining what incompetent means
jokes.punchline: Bart thinks the accurate description sounds positive
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Bart's loyalty and naivety, plus accurate description of Homer

jokes.text: you know, I defy anyone to tell the difference between these doughnuts and ones baked today.
jokes.setup: Homer defending old doughnuts
jokes.punchline: Implication he's eating very stale doughnuts while claiming they're fresh
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer self-deception and gluttony in one line

jokes.text: Come on, Simpson. If they wanted the kids to see you sitting around on your butt and stuffin' your face, they'd take them on a tour of your house.
jokes.setup: Coworker criticizing Homer's work habits
jokes.punchline: Implies Homer acts the same way at home as at work
jokes.explanation: Sharp insult that accurately captures Homer's character consistency

jokes.text: All right. Who's responsible for this? I might have known it was you, Simpson.
jokes.setup: Burns investigating accident
jokes.punchline: Immediately blames Homer without investigation
jokes.explanation: Establishes Burns' immediate hostility toward Homer and hasty judgment

jokes.text: Supervising technician at the toxic waste dump. / I'm no supervising technician. I'm a technical supervisor.
jokes.setup: Marge reading job listing
jokes.punchline: Homer making meaningless distinction between nearly identical titles
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of workplace bureaucracy and Homer's misplaced pride in meaningless distinctions

jokes.text: You've caused plenty of industrial accidents, and you've always bounced back.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to encourage Homer
jokes.punchline: Her encouragement is based on his history of causing disasters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - supportive but accidentally revealing Homer's incompetence

jokes.text: Is Mr. Freely there? / Who? / Freely. First initials 'I.P.' / Hold on. I'll check. Uh, is I.P. Freely here?
jokes.setup: Bart making prank call asking for someone
jokes.punchline: Name sounds like 'I pee freely' when said aloud
jokes.explanation: Classic prank call setup with perfect execution and timing. Became iconic Simpsons moment.

jokes.text: You'll get that punk someday, Moe. / Ah, I don't know. He's tough to catch. He keeps changing his name.
jokes.setup: Customer encouraging Moe to catch prank caller
jokes.punchline: Moe doesn't realize it's always the same kid with fake names
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moe's lack of awareness about the recurring pranks

jokes.text: You think you can still do that kind of work? / Sure. You never forget. It's just like riding a bicycle.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Marge returning to work
jokes.punchline: Homer comparing waitressing to riding a bike (sets up visual gag)
jokes.explanation: Setup for visual payoff showing Marge struggling as waitress

jokes.text: All he does is lie there like an unemployed whale.
jokes.setup: Bart describing Homer's depression
jokes.punchline: Specific and unflattering comparison to whale
jokes.explanation: Brilliant simile that perfectly captures Homer's state while being hilariously specific

jokes.text: There's only one thing we can do: take advantage of the old guy.
jokes.setup: Lisa proposing solution to Homer's depression
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact suggestion to exploit their depressed father
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Lisa's pragmatic side and family dysfunction

jokes.text: Duff, the beer that makes the days fly by.
jokes.setup: Beer slogan
jokes.punchline: Promoting alcohol as way to skip through life
jokes.explanation: Dark slogan that reveals the escapist nature of alcoholism

jokes.text: Beer. Now there's a temporary solution.
jokes.setup: Homer responding to beer commercial
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging beer won't solve his problems but embracing it anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - self-aware but still making bad choices. Became quotable line.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. Let me count and make sure. Not even close.
jokes.setup: Homer second-guessing his math about the piggy bank money
jokes.punchline: Confirms it's even worse than he thought
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the previous joke, showing Homer's methodical disappointment

jokes.text: Oh, looks like young Simpson is going to kill himself. Well, maybe not. Maybe he's just taking his boulder for a walk.
jokes.setup: Someone observing Homer with rock tied around him
jokes.punchline: Absurd alternative explanation for obvious suicide attempt
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor treating boulder-walking as normal alternative to suicide

jokes.text: Kill myself? Killing myself is the last thing I'd ever do.
jokes.setup: Family asking if Homer was trying to kill himself
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation - it would be the last thing since he'd be dead
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on literal and figurative levels, typical Homer logic

jokes.text: Cool, man.
jokes.setup: Bart seeing police sketch of 'El Barto'
jokes.punchline: Casually admiring the wanted poster of himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart reaction showing his pride in criminal reputation

jokes.text: boys and girls, retired people with nothing better to do.
jokes.setup: Homer addressing town meeting
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment of who attends local government meetings
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about civic engagement and who shows up to meetings

jokes.text: If they think I'm gonna stop at that stop sign, they're sadly mistaken.
jokes.setup: Homer's stop sign being approved
jokes.punchline: Random driver immediately stating they'll ignore it
jokes.explanation: Perfect cynical punchline undermining Homer's achievement and commenting on civic compliance

jokes.text: Now people won't be caught off guard by that little 'mm-mmm' in the road.
jokes.setup: Marge praising Homer's road improvement work
jokes.punchline: Her sound effect description of a minor road bump
jokes.explanation: Charming Marge moment with her innocent way of describing minor road hazards

jokes.text: Thank you. Unlike most of you, I am not a nut.
jokes.setup: Homer addressing crowd at safety rally
jokes.punchline: Insulting his audience while asking for their support
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer foot-in-mouth moment with terrible crowd work

jokes.text: That's Homer Simpson, sir. He used to work here in the plant, but we fired him for gross incompetence.
jokes.setup: Burns asking about Homer
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact explanation of why Homer was fired
jokes.explanation: Sets up irony of Burns wanting to rehire the man he fired for incompetence

jokes.text: Our lives are at the hands of men no smarter than you or I, many of them, incompetent boobs.
jokes.setup: Homer criticizing nuclear plant workers
jokes.punchline: Unknowingly describing himself accurately
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Homer accurately describes himself while thinking he's different

jokes.text: Gee, this guy's desk sure is big. I can't let Marge support the family. This guy's got the cleanest shirt I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Homer's internal monologue during job decision
jokes.punchline: Stream of consciousness observations instead of logical decision-making
jokes.explanation: Good insight into Homer's scattered thought process and superficial observations

jokes.text: Mmm, what the hey. I'll take the job.
jokes.setup: Homer's elaborate internal debate
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of important job decision
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer casual approach to major life decisions

jokes.text: you mean, you're willing to give up a good job and a raise just for your principles? / Mmm. When you put it that way, it does sound a little farfetched, but that's the lug you're lookin' at!
jokes.setup: Burns questioning Homer's principled stance
jokes.punchline: Homer admits his principles sound ridiculous but sticks to them
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's rare moral stance despite acknowledging it seems foolish

jokes.text: Of course, I'd have a lot less of those free minutes if you gave me the job.
jokes.setup: Homer vowing to spend free time on safety crusade
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by noting job would reduce free time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic undermining his own principled speech with practical consideration

jokes.text: Mmm. You're not as stupid as you look or sound or our best testing indicates.
jokes.setup: Burns complimenting Homer's cleverness
jokes.punchline: Escalating insults disguised as compliment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating insult structure that gets progressively more elaborate and devastating

jokes.text: you have to learn that there's a little Homer Simpson in all of us.
jokes.setup: Homer giving inspirational speech
jokes.punchline: Suggests everyone has his incompetence inside them
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of inspirational speech trope - saying everyone has his flaws is not encouraging

tmdb_overview: After being fired from the nuclear power plant for negligence, Homer becomes a safety activist.

guest_stars.name: Russi Taylor
guest_stars.character: Sherri / Terri / Inanimate Carbon Rod #1 / Mrs. Winfield (voice)

insight: First early Simpsons gem: 44 jokes in 22 minutes hits stride with character-driven irony.
